Expansionary fiscal policy is a form of macroeconomic policy that seeks to encourage economic growth by increasing aggregate demand through a combination of increased government spending and tax cuts. Cutting corporate taxes puts more money into the hands of companies which would be diverted into new projects and growing employment. Hence, it does not contribute to increasing the cost of production and thereby cost-push inflation. So, point I is not correct.
. Increase in interest rates e.g .- Bank Rate, Repo Rate, etc. by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) leads to increase in lending rates by the commercial banks which results in an increase in the borrowing cost of funds for the firms. This results in an increase in the cost-push inflation. So, point 2 is correct.
. Increase in minimum wages will increase the cost of production for firms as the cost of labour will increase. This would lead to cost -push inflation in the economy. So, point 3 is not correct.
. Appreciation of the rupee results in cheap imported commodities e.g .- crude oil, various raw materials, etc. It will reduce production costs and cost-push inflation.
So, point 4 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
It is the practice of reducing the size or quantity of a product while the price of the product remains
the same or slightly increases. Instead of increasing the price of a product, something that would be immediately evident to consumers, producers reduce the size of the product while maintaining the same price. This is the most likely description of:

so.t:
. Shrinkflation is the practice of reducing the size or quantity of a product while the price of the product remains the same or slightly increases. Essentially, shrinkflation is a form of hidden inflation. Instead of increasing the price of a product, something that would be immediately evident to consumers, producers reduce the size of the product while maintaining the same price. The absolute price of the product doesn't go up, but the price per unit of weight or volume has increased.
. Skimpflation is a situation where firms - in response to higher costs - reduce the quality of a good/service. It means consumers get less for the same price. It is related to inflation in that rising prices and costs in the economy lead to lower living standards for consumers. The difference is that although prices do not rise directly, consumers effectively have to spend more to get the same service that they did before.
. Skewflation is the relative increase in price of one or a group of commodities, and not the general rise in price level of all goods. Thus, it refers to the skewness of inflation among different sectors of the economy - some sectors are facing huge inflation, some none and some are facing deflation.
. Stagflation is an economic cycle characterised by slow growth and a high unemployment rate accompanied by inflation. It happens when prices are rising, currency is losing value, and no real growth in the economy is occurring to create jobs. Stagflation can happen if there is a recession before the rate of inflation has gone down to where central banks want it to be.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to ‘imported inflation’ in India:
1. It is a general and sustainable price increase due to an increase in the costs of imported products.
2. A weaker domestic currency stimulates exports and makes imports more expensive.
3. It leads to an increase in the prices of domestically produced goods.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
Imported Inflation:
. When the general price level rises in a country because of the rise in prices of imported commodities, it is called imported inflation. The exchange rate has an effect on the trade surplus or deficit, which in turn affects the exchange rate, and so on. In general, a weaker domestic currency stimulates exports and makes imports more expensive. Conversely, a strong domestic currency hampers exports and makes imports cheaper. So, statements I and 2 are correct.
. A large chunk of Indian imports consists of items which have an overall impact on the production of goods. A general and sustainable price hike in the prices of imported fuels, materials and components increases domestic costs of production. This results in increases in the prices of domestically produced goods. So, statement 3 is
correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Expansionary fiscal policy
2. Lowering tax rates
3. Currency devaluation
4. Raising interest rates
How many of the above steps can be taken to control demand-pull inflation?

so.t:
· Demand-pull inflation causes upward pressure on prices due to shortages in supply, a condition that economists describe as "too many dollars chasing too few goods." An increase in aggregate demand can also lead to this type of inflation. To counter demand and pull inflation, governments, and central banks would have to implement a tight monetary and fiscal policy.
· Contractionary policy is a monetary measure referring either to a reduction in government spending-particularly deficit spending or a reduction in the rate of monetary expansion by a central bank. For Example: lowering government spending by limiting subsidies, raising taxes and limiting the money supply in the economy. An increase in the interest rate would make consumers spend less on durable goods and housing. This would help to reduce demand-pull inflation in the market. So, point I is not correct.
. Tax cuts will leave more money at consumers' end (due to more disposable income) which will result in higher purchasing power and a rise in demand. This is likely to increase demand-pull inflation. So, point 2 is not correct.
. The rising interest rate decreases the money supply in the economy. The central bank uses this tool to control the money supply and credit. It is costly to borrow money in the economy and it leads to a decreased demand in the market and thus, controls inflation. So, point 4 is correct.
. Currency devaluation will lead to a further increase in inflation and imports will be costly especially crude oil, which will impact domestic supply. So, point 3 is not correct. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Decrease in purchasing power of money
2. Depletion in the saving rate in the long run in the economy
3. Increase in the volume of exports
How many of the above are the most likely effects of inflation?

so.t:
. Inflation occurs when there is a general increase in the price of goods and services and a fall in purchasing power. Purchasing power is the value of a currency expressed in the number of goods and services that one unit of the currency can purchase.
. An increase in inflation increases the 'nominal' value of income, while the 'real' value of income remains the same. If prices increase, so does the cost of living. A rise in inflation means an increase in the cost of goods and services. This is called an increase in the cost of living. This also means that the same amount of money cannot buy you the same goods and services in the future. This is called a reduction in the purchasing power of money. So, point I is correct.
. Holding money does not remain an intelligent economic decision (because money loses value with every increase in inflation) which is why people try to put maximum money with the banks in their savings accounts. Prices tend to rise over time and that reduces the value of your savings. In the long-run, higher inflation depletes the saving rate in an economy. So, point 2 is correct.
. With every inflation, the currency of the economy depreciates. The exportable items of an economy gain competitive prices in the world market. Due to this, the volume of export increases (however, the value of export decreases here) and thus export income increases in the economy. It means an export segment of the economy benefits due to inflation. Importing partners of the economy exert pressure for a stable exchange rate as their imports start increasing and exports start decreasing. So, point 3 is correct.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Increase in the tax revenue of the government
2. Decline in the Non-Performing Assets of banks
3. Decline in employment
4. Decrease in aggregate demand
How many of the above are the most likely impacts of the recession on an economy?

so.t:
A recession can be defined as a sustained period of weak or negative growth in real GDP (output) that is accompanied by a significant rise in the unemployment rate.
. When a recession occurs, the economy contracts and the unemployment rate likely increases. The tax revenue of the government falls due to fall in income in the economy. In this case, the government might pursue expansionary fiscal policy to encourage the economy to expand, or grow. The government could decrease tax rates. So, point I is not correct.
. During a recession, the numbers of households and businesses that are unable to pay back loans are unusually high, as is the number of businesses that close down. This leads to an increase in the Non-Performing Assets of banks. So, point 2 is not correct.
. Recession decreases employment in an economy. Companies lay off employees as output declines. Since a recession denotes a decline in economic activity and labour is a key economic input alongside capital, it stands to reason that employment must decline as output drops. So, point 3 is correct.
. A recession indicates declining aggregate demand and hence the output. This indicates comparatively lower purchasing capacity among consumers. A recession is a sustained period of weak or negative growth in real GDP (output). So, point 4 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the call money market, consider the following:
1. The scheduled commercial banks act as both the borrowers and lenders in the money market.
2. A tight liquidity condition leads to a fall in the call money rate.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
. Statement I is correct: The call money market serves as an interbank platform for short-term borrowing and lending, typically lasting for one day. Banks utilize these transactions to address asset-liability mismatches, fulfill statutory CRR and SLR mandates, and address sudden fund demands. Scheduled commercial banks and cooperative banks engage as both borrowers and lenders in this market, while entities like LIC, GIC, Mutual Funds, and NABARD are limited to lending roles.
. Statement 2 is incorrect: The call money rate is affected by the demand and supply of liquidity in the market. A tight liquidity condition leads to a rise in call money rate and vice versa.
. Therefore, option a is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to Participatory-notes (P-notes), consider the following statements:
1. These can be issued by Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) only to those overseas investors that are
registered with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).
2. These are offshore derivative instruments with Indian shares as underlying assets.
Which of the statements given above is/ are correct?

so.t:
. Participatory-notes or P-notes are issued by registered foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) to overseas investors who wish to be a part of the Indian stock market without registering themselves directly. They, however, need to go through a due diligence process.
· P-notes are used by foreign funds, foreign institutional investors (FIls) and investors, such as hedge funds who are not registered with Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) but are interested in exposure to Indian securities. So, statement I is not correct.
. Participatory notes are offshore derivative instruments with Indian shares as underlying assets. Brokers and foreign institutional investors registered with the (SEBI) issue the participatory notes and invest on behalf of the foreign investors. Brokers must report their participatory note issuance status to the regulatory board each quarter. The notes allow foreign investors with high net worth, hedge funds, and other investors, to participate in the Indian markets without registering with the SEBI. Investors save time, money and scrutiny associated with direct registration. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to Inflation-indexed Bonds, consider the following statements:
1. They are tied to the costs of consumer goods as measured by an inflation index.
2. They are tradable in the secondary market like other government securities.
3. They cannot be used by banks to maintain statutory liquidity ratio (SLR).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
. Inflation Indexed Bonds (IIBs) provide inflation protection to both principal and interest payments. Inflation-linked bonds are tied to the costs of consumer goods as measured by an inflation index. They can help to hedge against inflation risk because they increase in value during inflationary periods. So, statement I is correct.
. As IIBs are G-Sec, they can be tradable in the secondary market like other G-Secs. Investors will be able to trade them in the OTC market, and stock exchanges. So, statement 2 is correct.
. IIBs are G-Sec and issued as part of the approved Government market borrowing programme. Therefore, IIBs can be used by banks for statutory liquidity ratio (SLR). So, statement 3 is not correct.

q.t:
It is an unsecured money market instrument issued in the form of a promissory note. It is a short-term
instrument and the maturity period varies from seven days to up to one year. It can be issued by corporates, primary dealers, and financial institutions.
Which one of the following financial instruments best reflects the above description?

so.t:
. Money market is the short-term financial market of an economy. In this market, money is traded between individuals or groups (i.e., financial institutions, banks, government, companies, etc.), who are either cash-surplus or cash-scarce. There are several instruments in the money market.
. Commercial Paper (CP) is an unsecured money market instrument issued in the form of a promissory note that was introduced in 1990. CPs are short-term instruments and the maturity period varies from seven days to up to one year.
The buyer gets CP at a discounted rate, while he gets face value on maturity. CPs can be issued by corporates, primary dealers, and financial institutions. In October 2019, the SEBI allowed listing of the Commercial Papers to broaden investor participation. It is used by the corporate houses in India (which should be a listed company with a working capital of not less than ₹5 crore). The CP issuing companies need to obtain a specified credit rating from an agency approved by the RBI (such as CRISIL, ICRA, etc).

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Security and Exchange Board of India (SEBI):
1. It is a statutory body.
2. All board members are nominated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
3. It overlooks registration and regulation of mutual funds and venture capital funds.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
. The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) was initially set up as a non- statutory body in 1988 through a government resolution in an effort to give the Indian stock market an organized structure. Later, it became autonomous and given statutory powers through the SEBI Act, 1992. So, statement I is correct.
. The SEBI Board comprises nine members excluding the chairman- one member each from Ministries of Finance and Law, one member nominated by the Reserve Bank of India and two other members appointed by central government. It has 4 full time members (including the chairman). All members, except one nominated by the RBI, are
appointed by the Central Government. So, statement 2 is not correct.
. The major objective of SEBI is to regulate the securities market in India and protect the interests of investors in securities. It also overlooks registration and regulation of mutual funds and venture capital funds. So, statement 3 is correct.
· SEBI has quasi-legislative and quasi-judicial powers. It can draft regulations, conduct inquiries, pass rulings and impose penalties. In case of frauds and unethical practices pertaining to the securities market, it has the power to pass judgments.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to Red Herring Prospectus (RHP):
1. It is a preliminary prospectus, usually in connection with the company’s initial public offering (IPO).
2. It includes key details of the security issue, such as its price and the number of shares offered.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
. A red herring is a preliminary prospectus filed by a company with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), usually in connection with the company's initial public offering (IPO). A red herring prospectus contains most of the information pertaining to the company's operations and prospects but does not include key details of the security issue, such as its price and the number of shares offered. Hence, Statement I is correct and Statement 2 is incorrect.
. Once the registration statement becomes effective, the company disseminates a final prospectus that contains the final IPO price and issue size. Expressions of interest then convert to orders for the issue at the buyer's option. The minimum period between a registration statement filing and its effective date is 15 days.
. Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following option:
1. Interest Rates Hikes
2. Quantitative easing
3. Dovish monetary policy
4. Reducing reverse repo rate
How many of the above given options should the Reserve Bank of India take to curb ‘Demand Pull Inflation’?

so.t:
. To curb "Demand Pull Inflation," which occurs when aggregate demand exceeds aggregate supply, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) typically employs measures to reduce demand pressure in the economy.
. Interest Rates Hikes: Increasing interest rates is a common tool used by central banks to reduce borrowing and spending, thereby reducing aggregate demand. It makes borrowing more expensive, which tends to slow down economic activity. So, this option is effective in curbing demand-pull inflation. Hence, option I is the desired option.
· Quantitative Easing: Quantitative easing involves purchasing government securities or other securities from the market to increase money supply and lower interest rates. It is usually used to stimulate economic activity during periods of low inflation or recession. However, in the context of curbing demand-pull inflation, it may not be the most appropriate tool as it increases money supply, potentially exacerbating inflationary pressures.
. Dovish Monetary Policy (cheap money policy): A dovish monetary policy involves measures aimed at stimulating economic growth, often by lowering interest rates and increasing money supply. This policy stance is typically adopted during periods of economic slowdown or recession. It is not suitable for curbing demand-pull inflation as it encourages borrowing and spending, thereby potentially exacerbating inflation.
. Reducing Reverse Repo Rate: The reverse repo rate is the rate at which the central bank borrows money from commercial banks. By reducing the reverse repo rate, the central bank encourages commercial banks to lend more money to the market, thereby increasing liquidity in the market. This measure cannot help in curbing excess demand in the economy, making it ineffective in combating demand-pull inflation.
. Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Sr. No
 Type of Inflation
 Description
1.
 Deflation
 Decrease in the rate of inflation over time
2.
 Skimpflation
 Firms, in response to higher costs, reduce the quality of a good/service
3.
 Disinflation
 Sustained decrease in the general price
4.
 Hyperinflation
 Rapid increase in the general price of goods and services
How many of the above mentioned pairs are correctly matched?

so.t:
. Deflation: Deflation refers to a sustained decrease in the general price level of goods and services in an economy. It is the opposite of inflation. During deflation, the purchasing power of money increases over time, meaning that goods and services become cheaper. Hence, pair I is not correctly matched.
· Skimpflation: Skimpflation is a situation where firms - in response to higher costs - reduce the quality of a good/service. It means consumers get less for the same price. It is related to inflation in that rising prices and costs in the economy lead to lower living standards for consumers. The difference is that although prices do not rise directly, consumers effectively have to spend more to get the same service that they did before. Hence, pair 2 is correctly matched.
. Disinflation: Disinflation refers to a decrease in the rate of inflation over time. It does not mean prices are falling, but rather that the rate at which prices are increasing is slowing down. Disinflation often occurs as a result of deliberate monetary policy actions taken by central banks to stabilise the economy and control inflation. Hence, pair 3 is not correctly matched.
. Hyperinflation: Hyperinflation is an extremely rapid and out-of-control increase in the general price level of goods and services within an economy. It is characterised by prices doubling or even tripling in a very short period, usually less than a year. Hence, pair 4 is correctly matched.
. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the statements regarding wholesale price index (WPI) and Consumer price index (CPI):
1. CPI and WPI represent the inflation of imported goods also.
2. CPI does not represent the inflation in capital and intermediate goods.
3. Housing, Fuel and Light comprises the largest share of CPI.
How many of the above mentioned statements are correct?

so.t:
. When goods are imported in India, first they move to the wholesale mandis and then they come in the retail markets. So, wholesale prices and retail prices both get impacted because of the imported goods. Hence, statement I is correct
. The goods purchased by consumers in the retail market do not represent all the goods produced in the country (like inputs, intermediates and capital goods are purchased by the companies), so CPI does not include these items but WPI takes into account all such goods. Hence, statement 2 is correct.
· CPI is calculated for a fixed list of items including food, housing, apparel, transportation, electronics, medical care, education, etc. The weightage of food and beverages in the CPI is 45.86%. Fuel and Housing comprises only 16%. Hence, statement 3 is incorrect

opt.t: Shifting of Administrative office of Multi National Company (MNCs)to a Tax Haven country.
Creating a Data centre in Country from where data is being sourced.
Setting up a Cluster workspace for MSMEs in a central business district.
Associated with a facility where a third party can lease a rack/server space along with other computer hardware

q.t: Recently, the term “Co-location” was seen in the news. It is related to:

so.t:
. Multiple agencies are investigating the NSE co-location scam related to the manipulation of the market at the stock exchange, in which several former top officials including former CEOs Chitra Ramakrishna and Ravi Narain are under a cloud.
. Co-location is typically associated with a facility where a third party can lease a rack/server space along with other computer hardware. Co-location facility provides infrastructure such as power supply, bandwidth, and cooling for setting up servers and storage of data.

q.t:
With reference to India’s infrastructure development, consider the following statements:
1. The Golden Quadrilateral aims to connect India's four largest metropolises with high density traffic corridors.
2. The North-South Corridor is the longest compare to the East-West Corridor
3. The East-West Corridor is a long route connecting Silchar in Assam to Porbandar in Gujarat.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: The Golden Quadrilateral is indeed a project connecting India's four largest metropolises with high-density traffic corridors, significantly reducing travel time and cost.
● Statement 2 is correct: The North-South Corridor spans 4,076 kilometers and is longer than the East-West corridor.
● Statement 3 is correct: The East-West Corridor does span 3,640 kilometers, connecting Silchar in Assam to Porbandar in Gujarat
● Golden quadrilateral: The Golden Quadrilateral is a 5,846 km long, 4/6 lane, high-density traffic corridor being built to connect India's four largest metropolises:
○ Delhi-Mumbai 1419 km
○ Mumbai-Chennai 1290km
○ Kolkata-Chennai 1684km
○ Delhi-Kolkata 1453km
● Outcome: The time-distance and cost of travel between India's megacities will be greatly reduced with the
building of the Golden Quadrilateral.
● North-South: A 4,076 km long route known as the North-South Corridor would link Kanyakumari, Tamil
Nadu, and Kochchi-Salem Spur in Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir.
● East-West Corridor: The East-West Corridor is a 3,640-kilometer-long route that would connect Silchar
in Assam to Porbandar in Gujarat

q.t:
With reference to the Bharatmala Pariyojana, consider the following statements:
1. The development of the Green Field Expressway is the primary focus under this initiative to ensure better traffic and freight management.
2. The funding for Bharatmala will rely solely on government budgetary allocation due to the inadequacy of other financial sources.
3. Economic Corridors, Inter Corridors, National Corridors, and International Connectivity are distinct components under the Bharatmala Pariyojana.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: The Bharatmala Pariyojana emphasizes building and developing the Green Field Expressway to enhance traffic and freight management
● Statement 2 is incorrect: The funding sources for Bharatmala include market borrowings, the Central road fund, monetizing government-owned road assets, and budgetary allocation
● Statement 3 is correct: Economic Corridors, Inter Corridors, National Corridors, and International Connectivity are distinct components specified under the Bharatmala Pariyojana,
Bharatmala Pariyojana:
● Launched by: The Indian government introduced the "Bharatmala Pariyojana," a new umbrella initiative for the roadways sector.
● Objective: focuses on improving the efficiency of road traffic circulation throughout the country by filling essential infrastructural gaps.
● Bharatmala Route: The government will raise funds for Bharatmala through four distinct channels:
○ Market borrowings
○ Central road fund
○ Monetizing government-owned road assets
○ Budgetary allocation
● Features of Bharatmala Pariyojana:
○ Enhance road condition: The major goal was to enhance road condition in order to spur growth in
every region of the country.
○ Development of new highway: Another key aspect of this scheme's announcement is the
development of new highways.
○ Building roads: The government intends to complete the building of all roads under this project
within five years.
○ Funding: The Ministry is relying on other sources to complete the project because the government
funding may not be adequate
● Bharatmala Project Components:
○ Economic Corridor: According to the parameters of the road development project, the federal government will construct 9000 kilometres of Economic Corridors.
○ Inter Corridor: The overall length of the highways classified as Feeder Routes or Inter Corridors is 6000 kilometres.
○ Enhancement of efficiency: 5000 kilometres of roads built under the project would be designated as National Corridors to improve road connectivity.
○ International connectivity: The project calls for the construction of 2000 kilometres of border roads or international connectivity roads to connect cities and distant locations.
○ Port connectivity: The central government has directed the development of 2000 kilometres of highways to connect the coast and ports.
○ Green field expressway: The main focus will be on the building and development of the Green Field Expressway for better traffic and freight management.

q.t:
Which of the following statements regarding the Sagarmala Project is/are correct?
1. One of the aims of the Sagarmala project is skill development of the community living around the coastal region.
2. One of the components of the Sagarmala Programme includes creating Coastal Economic Zones.
3. The Sagarmala Project does not involve efforts towards enhancing connectivity of ports to the hinterland which is under Bharatmala project.
How many of the above statements are incorrect?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct. The Sagarmala Project encompasses multiple components, including port modernization, connectivity enhancement, industrialization, and coastal community development.
● Statement 2 is correct; it involves creating Coastal Economic Zones to boost economic activity.
● Statement 3 is incorrect as enhancing connectivity to the hinterland is a key component for efficient cargo
movement under the Sagarmala Project.
Sagarmala Project:
● Launched: In 2015, the Union Cabinet launched the Sagarmala Program.
● Objective: The Sagarmala Project intends to cut logistical costs for EXIM and local commerce while investing in minimum infrastructure.
● Estimation: Sagarmala could boost India's exports to USD 110 billion and create 10 million new jobs by 2025.
● Components of the Sagarmala Programme:
○ Port Modernization: removing bottlenecks, increasing port capacity, and creating new, greenfield ports.
○ Enhancing connectivity of ports: to the hinterland, optimizing cost and time of cargo movement through multi-modal logistics solutions.
○ Industrialization at ports: creating Coastal Economic Zones and industry clusters near ports to save back on logistics expenses.
○ Coastal Community Development: Coastal communities should promote sustainable development through skill development, livelihood generation, fisheries, and tourism.
○ Inland Waterways Transport: encouragement for using inland and coastal rivers for environmentally benign and sustainable cargo transportation

q.t:
With respect to Vande Bharat Trains, Consider the following statements:
1. The primary advantage of Vande Bharat Trains lies in their reliance on traditional locomotives so that they can run on existing tracks.
2. Train 18 was an alternate name given to the Vande Bharat during its development phase.
3. Vande-Bharat train services are connecting only those states having Board Gauge (BG) electrified network.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is incorrect: The advantage of Vande Bharat Trains lies in their operation without traditional locomotives, as they are self-propelled trains, not reliant on traditional locomotives.
● Statement 2 is correct: the Vande Bharat Trains were referred to as Train 18 during their development phase.
● Statement 3 is correct: Vande-Bharat train services are connecting only those states having Board Gauge (BG) electrified network..
Vande Bharat Trains:
● About: It is an indigenously designed and manufactured semi high speed, self-propelled train
○ It is the next major leap: for the Indian Railways in terms of speed and passenger convenience since the introduction of Rajdhani trains.
● The first Vande Bharat: was manufactured by the Integral Coach Factory (ICF), Chennai, as part of the ‘Make in India’ programme, at a cost of about Rs. 100 crore.
● Advantages: The train set configuration, though complex, is faster, easier to maintain, consumes less energy, and has greater flexibility in train operation.
● Features of the Vande Bharat Trains:
○ New technology: These trains, dubbed as Train 18 during the development phase, operate without a locomotive
 They are based on: a propulsion system called distributed traction power technology, by which each car of the train set is powered.
○ Facilities: Its coaches incorporate passenger amenities including
 On-board WiFi entertainment,
 GPS-based passenger information system,
 CCTVs,
 Automatic doors in all coaches,
 Bio-vacuum type toilets like in aircraft.
○ Speed: It can achieve a maximum speed of 160 kmph due to faster acceleration and deceleration, reducing journey time by 25% to 45%.
○ New braking technology: It also has an intelligent braking system with power regeneration for better energy efficiency thereby making it cost, energy and environment efficient (KAVACH).

q.t:
Consider the following statements related to the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP):
1. The majority of the projected capital in the NIP is primarily allocated to the energy sector.
2. The NIP aims to source approximately 50% of its investment from the private sector.
3. Over the period from 2020 to 2025, the Central Government will contribute remaining 50% of the total investment envisaged.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: While energy is a significant sector in NIP, the major sectors are Energy (24%), Roads (18%), Urban (17%), and Railways (12%), together accounting for over 70% of the projected capital.
● Statement 2 is incorrect: The NIP anticipates 21% investment from the private sector initially, expected to increase to 30% by 2025.
● Statement 3 is incorrect: The Central Government's share in investment for NIP is 39%, not 50%.
The National Infrastructure Pipeline
● About: The NIP has projected total infrastructure investment of Rs 111 lakh crore during the period FY 2020 to 2025 in India.
● Major sectors: Energy (24%), Roads (18%), Urban (17%), and Railways 12%) along take up more than 70% of the projected capital.
● Source of Investment: NIP envisages, Central Government (39%) and State Government (40%) share.
○ Private: The remaining 21% will come from private sector.
○ It is also expected that the private sector share will increase to 30% in 2025.

q.t:
Consider the following statements related to the PM Gati Shakti:
1. The Gati Shakti plan aims to unite all infrastructural initiatives of various ministries through individual portals.
2. One of the main goals of Gati Shakti is to enhance data-driven decision-making through GIS-based analytical tools.
3. The National Logistics Policy of 2022 is a part of the PM Gati Shakti plan's subsumed projects.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is incorrect: PM Gati Shakti aims to centralize all infrastructural initiatives through a single portal, not individual portals.
● Statement 2 is correct: Gati Shakti intends to enable data-driven decisions via GIS-based tools, facilitating better visibility and analytical capabilities.
● Statement 3 is incorrect: The National Logistics Policy of 2022 is separate from PM Gati Shakti, even though both aim to enhance logistics and infrastructure.
PM Gati Shakti
● About: PM Gati Shakti plan envisages a centralised portal to unite all ministry's infrastructural initiatives.
● Targets: to cut logistic costs, increase cargo handling capacity and reduce the turnaround time.
● Aim: It is a campaign to lend more speed (Gati) and power (Shakti) to projects by connecting all concerned
departments on one platform.
● National Infrastructure pipeline: the 111 lakh crore project is now subsumed under the Gati shakti.
● Pillars of Gati Shakti:
○ Comprehensiveness:
 One portal: It will include all the existing and planned initiatives of various Ministries and Departments.
 Know each other: Each and every Department will now have visibility of each other’s activities.
○ Prioritization:
 Project prioritization: Through this, different Departments will be able to prioritize their projects.
○ Optimization:
 Planning: The National Master Plan will assist different ministries in planning for projects after
identification of critical gaps.
 Example: For the transportation of the goods from one place to another, the plan will help in selecting the
most optimum route in terms of time and cost.

q.t:
With reference to Bibek Debroy Committee's recommendations, consider the following statements:
1. Implementing the Bibek Debroy Committee's suggestion to rationalize fare structures and subsidies in the railways aims to enhance the competitiveness of freight tariffs, potentially shifting modal shares from road to rail transportation.
2. The absence of an Independent Regulator for Railways may impede the goal of ensuring a market-determined and competitive tariff system.
3. The absence of comprehensive accounting systems in the Railways significantly hampers cost assessment and evaluation of project viability.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
 Statement 1 is correct: Rationalizing fare structures and subsidies, as proposed by the Bibek Debroy Committee, aims to end cross-subsidization in railways. This will make freight tariffs competitive with road transportation costs, potentially increasing Railways' modal share.
 Statement 2 is correct: The Committee proposed an Independent Regulator for Railways to monitor tariffs, ensuring market competitiveness. Absence of such regulation might hinder the competitiveness of the tariff system.
 Statement 3 is correct: The absence of a robust accounting system in Railways hampers cost assessment and project viability evaluation. The current system lacks details on various activities and services' costs, making it challenging to assess project benefits accurately.
Bibek Debroy Committee Recommendations:
 Rationalize fare structures and subsidies: by ending the cross-subsidisation model presently followed by railways.
o Freight tariffs: should be competitive with the cost of road transportation so that there is increase in the modal share of Railways.
 Independent Regulator for Railways to: determine the tariff and monitor whether the tariff is market determined and competitive.
 Focus on Core Activities: of running trains and give upon-core activities such as running schools, hospitals and a police force.
 Accounting reforms: The current accounting system does not provide details of the cost of various activities and services, such as introduction of new trains and scheduling of stops.
o Issue: It neither tracks assets nor assesses liabilities.
o Difficulty: Consequently, it becomes difficult to compute the costs and benefits of any project or
activity.
o Need: Hence, in this regard, there is a need to adopt accounting reforms to track these details.
 Financing of Projects: Railways can lease huge amount of land that it holds to the private sector for certain duration of time and earn revenue.
o PPP: Similarly, railways can enter into PPP agreements for the development of stations.
 Safety of Railways: The Kakodkar committee had recommended for an investment of Rs 1 lakh crores over a period of 5 years to improve the safety of Indian railways.

q.t:
With respect to government efforts to boost solar manufacturing, consider the following statements:
1. India allows 100% foreign investment via automatic route in solar power manufacturing.
2. Foreign investors are encouraged to establish solar power plants under the BOOT (build-own-operate-transfer)
model.
3. There is no safeguard duty imposed on imports of solar equipment.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
 Statement 1 is correct: Government allows 100% foreign investment under automatic approval route.
 Statement 2 is correct: The government indeed encourages foreign investors to establish solar power plants
through the BOO model
 Statement 3 is incorrect: A safeguard duty of 25% has been imposed on imports of solar equipment, as a
measure to support domestic manufacturing

q.t:
With reference to the agriculture-related policies for inclusive growth, consider the following statements:
1. Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana aims at enhancing micro-irrigation and watershed development.
2. National Agriculture Market also focuses on establishing local mandis for crop procurement.
3. The National Food Security Act emphasizes crop diversification and land consolidation.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana aims at enhancing micro-irrigation and watershed development to improve farm productivity and water use efficiency.
● Statement 2 is incorrect: National Agriculture Market aims at creating a unified national market for agricultural produce by establishing an online trading platform, not establishment of local mandis.
● Statement 3 is incorrect: The National Food Security Act focuses on providing food security by ensuring access to adequate quantities of food at affordable prices to people, without emphasis on crop diversification or land consolidation.
● Key components and objectives of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana include:
o Accelerated Irrigation Benefit Program (AIBP): The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to states to expedite the completion of ongoing irrigation projects and create new irrigation infrastructure to increase the irrigated area.
o Har Khet Ko Pani: The PMKSY focuses on providing sustainable water supply to all agricultural lands and ensuring that every farm receives adequate irrigation, thereby enhancing productivity and crop diversification.
o Per Drop More Crop: Encouraging efficient water use through precision irrigation techniques, micro-irrigation, sprinkler and drip irrigation systems, and other water-saving technologies to ensure optimal utilization of available water resources.
o Watershed Development: Emphasizing the need for comprehensive watershed management to conserve rainwater, improve soil moisture retention, and enhance groundwater recharge, which aids in sustainable agriculture.
● Key features and objectives of the National Agriculture Market include:
o Unified National Market: NAM aims to establish a common nationwide electronic trading platform, leveraging technology to link Agriculture Produce Marketing Committees (APMCs) or mandis across states and regions.
o Transparent Price Discovery: It provides farmers with real-time information on prices prevailing in different markets, enabling them to make informed decisions about where to sell their produce for better remuneration.
o Reduced Middlemen Intervention: NAM intends to minimize the role of intermediaries, allowing farmers to directly access markets beyond their local mandis, thereby reducing transaction costs and enhancing their income.
o Online Trading: The platform facilitates online trading of agricultural produce, enabling farmers to showcase their products to buyers from various regions, promoting fair and competitive price discovery.

q.t:
With reference to the initiatives to reduce unemployment, consider statements:
1. Mahatma Gandhi the National following Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) aims to create efficient and effective institutional platforms for rural poor to enhance family income.
2. Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana (DAY) with an aim to uplift the urban poor folks by enhancing sustainable livelihood opportunities through skill development..
3. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) implemented by National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: MGNREGA aims to provide job opportunities and sustainable livelihoods to rural households.
● Statement 2 is incorrect: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana (DAY) with an aim to uplift the urban poor folks by enhancing sustainable livelihood opportunities through skill development. Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana was launched under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation (HUPA). The Government of India has provisioned Rs.500 crore for the scheme.
● Statement 3 is correct: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) implemented by National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC). The objective of this Skill Certification scheme is to enable Indian youth to take up industry relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood. Individuals with prior experience are also assessed and certified under Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL).
Key features and objectives of the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) include:
● Skill Training: PMKVY offers skill development training to youth across diverse sectors and industries based on the National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF).
● Short-Term Training: The scheme focuses on providing short-term training programs that typically last between 150 and 300 hours, ensuring swift learning and skill acquisition.
● Financial Support: Trainees enrolled in PMKVY receive financial rewards upon successful completion of the training programs. This monetary incentive is intended to encourage and recognize skill acquisition.
● Industry-Aligned Curriculum: The training programs are designed to meet the requirements of various industries, aligning with their skill demands and ensuring job readiness among trainees.

q.t:
With reference to the government schemes for poverty alleviation, consider the following statements:
1. Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awaas Yojana aims to provide low-interest loans and annual guaranteed wage work to rural households.
2. Annapurna provides 10 kg of free food grains to all eligible senior citizens, regardless of their registration under the National Old Age Pension Scheme.
3. The National Food Security Mission focuses on enhancing production of rice, wheat and pulses.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is incorrect: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awaas Yojana aims to build homes for rural households and offers low-interest loans, but it doesn't provide annual guaranteed wage work.
● Statement 2 is incorrect: Annapurna provides free food grains specifically to eligible senior citizens not registered under the National Old Age Pension Scheme.
● Statement 3 is correct: The National Food Security Mission aims to increase production of rice, wheat, pulses, and coarse cereals in identified districts.
Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awaas Yojana
● Objective: PMGAY primarily focuses on providing financial assistance to rural households living in inadequate housing conditions or those without proper housing.
● Beneficiaries: The scheme targets beneficiaries from marginalized sections of society such as SCs, STs, minorities, and other economically weaker sections in rural areas.
● Financial Assistance: Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries receive financial aid for the construction and upgradation of houses. The financial assistance is provided by the central and state governments in a cost-sharing pattern.
● Unit Assistance: The financial assistance is fixed per housing unit and is directly credited to the beneficiary's bank account in installments.

q.t:
Consider the following states:
1. West Bengal
2. Assam
3. Kerala
4. Sikkim
How many of the above are among the top five tea producing states in India?

so.t:
Tea plants grow well in tropical and subtropical climates. It grows well in deep and fertile well-drained soil, rich in humus and organic matter.
India is the second largest producer of tea and accounts for about 28 per cent of total production in the world. Indian Assam, Darjeeling, and Nilgiri tea are considered one of the finest in the world.
Major tea producing states in India are Assam, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka and Kerala.
Option 1 is correct. West Bengal is the second largest tea producing state in India. The state contributes more than 25% of India’s Tea Production.
Option 2 is correct. Assam is the largest tea producing state in India, accounting for over 50% of the country’s total tea production. Assam is the single largest tea-growing region in the world.
Option 3 is correct. Kerala is the fourth largest tea producing state in India.
Option 4 is incorrect. Sikkim produces tea but is not a major producer. Sikkim produces certain varieties of black tea, white tea, green tea, and Oolong tea.
Knowledge Base:
● Tea leaves have a rich content of caffeine and tannin.
● Black tea leaves are fermented whereas green tea leaves are unfermented.
● Tea cultivation is an example of plantation agriculture.
● Assam accounts for about 53.2 per cent of the total cropped area and contributes more than half of total production of tea in the country. West Bengal and Tamil Nadu are the other leading producers of tea.
● Climate and soil type:
○ The tea plant grows well in tropical and sub-tropical climates endowed with deep and fertile well-drained soil, rich in humus and organic matter.
○ Tea bushes require a warm and moist frost-free climate all through the year.
○ Frequent showers evenly distributed over the year ensure continuous growth of tender leaves.

q.t:
With reference to the production of sugarcane in India, which of the following statements are correct?
1. The total annual sugarcane production has steadily increased during the last five years.
2. The per hectare yield of sugarcane in peninsular India is generally higher than that of Northern states.
3. In 2023-24, Maharashtra was the largest sugarcane producing state in India.
4. India is the second largest producer of sugarcane in the world after Brazil.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Sugarcane is a crop of tropical areas. It is the main source of sugar, gur (jaggary), khandsari and molasses. It grows well in hot and humid climates with a temperature of 21°C to 27°C and an annual rainfall between 75cm and 100cm. Under rainfed conditions, it is cultivated in sub-humid and humid climates. Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu, are the leading producers of this crop.
Statement 1 is incorrect. The total annual sugarcane production was 344 Million Tonnes (MT) in 2019 20; 370.5 MT in 2020-21; 461.6 MT in 2021-22; 494.2 MT in 2022-23; and 453.2 in 2023-24. So we can say that total annual sugarcane production has not consistently increased during the last five years.
Statement 2 is correct. The per hectare yield level of sugarcane is higher in peninsular India compared to northern India. This is primarily due to the fact that peninsular states have a more tropical climate with longer sunshine hours, higher temperatures, and well-distributed rainfall, which are ideal for sugarcane growth. Sugarcane yield and sugar recovery from the cane in the South is considerably higher than the North.
Statement 3 is incorrect. Uttar Pradesh is the largest sugarcane producing state in India. Uttar Pradesh produces about two-fifths of sugarcane of the country. It is followed by Maharashtra and Karnataka.
Statement 4 is correct. Brazil is the largest producer of sugarcane in the world with more than 700 million tones of sugarcane. India ranks second after Brazil in terms of sugarcane production.

opt.t: procurement incidentals and charges for godowns.

q.t: The economic cost of food grains to the Food Corporation of India is Minimum Support Price and bonus (if any) paid to the farmers plus

so.t: The economic cost of food grains to the Food Corporation of India comprises three components. The procurement price of food grains, costs related to procurement (such as statutory taxes, labour costs, mandi fees, and so on) and costs of distribution (including freight, storage and administration).

opt.t: Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct and both of them explain Statement-I
Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement-I
Only one of the Statement II and III is correct and that explains Statement-I
Neither Statement-II nor Statement-III is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I: Rajasthan is the leading producer of Bajra amongst all the states in India.
Statement-II: Rajasthan has a hot and arid climate with low rainfall, suitable for the growth of Bajra.
Statement-III: Rajasthan has sandy soils that are ideal for the cultivation of Bajra.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
Exp) Option a is the correct answer.
Statement-I is correct: India is one of the leading producers of millets in the world with an estimated share of around 41 percent in the global production. Rajasthan is the leading producer of Bajra in the country followed by Uttar Pradesh, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh and Haryana.
Statement-II is correct and is the correct explanation of Statement-I: Bajra is sown in hot and dry climatic conditions in north-western and western parts of the country. It is a hardy crop that resists frequent dry spells and drought in the country.
Statement-III is correct and is the correct explanation of Statement-I: Bajra has a wide adaptability as it may grow under different day lengths, temperature and moisture stress. It requires low annual rainfall ranging between 40-50 cm and dry weather. The crop may tolerate drought but cannot withstand high rainfall of 90 cm or above. Light soils of low inherent fertility, good drainage, and mild salinity are the best types for this crop. Crop does not tolerate soil acidity. Rajasthan has predominantly sandy soils, which are considered ideal for growing Bajra due to its ability to thrive in well-drained, sandy conditions and tolerate arid climates.

q.t:
With reference to Fertiliser industry in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Union government allocates fertilisers to each state for each season.
2. Fertiliser companies can sell subsidized fertilisers directly to the farmers.
3. There is no limit on the sale of fertilizer to one person or one family.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Exp) Option d is the correct answer.
The fertiliser industry in India plays a crucial role in ensuring food security by supplying essential nutrients to crops. The Government of India regulates the fertiliser sector through subsidies and allocation mechanisms to make fertilisers affordable and accessible to farmers.
Statement 1 is correct: The Government of India/Union government determines the Recommended Dose of Fertiliser (RDF) based on the Recommended Dose of Nutrients (RDN), which is calculated by each state. Fertilisers are then allocated to states for each season. This allocation ensures an adequate supply based on regional soil and crop requirements.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The sale of all subsidized fertilizers to farmers/buyers is made through Point of Sale (PoS) devices. These are installed at each retailer shop and the beneficiaries are identified through Aadhaar Card, KCC, Voter Identity Card etc. Under the fertilizer DBT system, 100% subsidy on various fertilizer grades is released to the fertilizer companies on the basis of actual sales made by the retailers to the beneficiaries.
Statement 3 is correct: Currently, any individual possessing an Aadhaar card can purchase fertilisers in any quantity, whether they are farmers or not. This lack of restrictions has led to problems such as diversion for non-agricultural purposes, overuse harming soil health, and inefficiencies in subsidy allocation.

q.t:
With reference to agricultural systems, consider the following statements:
1. It is generally dominated by wet paddy cultivation or by crops like wheat, sorghum and barley.
2. Large holdings of land for agricultural activities are the general characteristics of this agricultural system.
3. Use of machinery is limited and most of the agricultural operations are done by manual labour in this agricultural system.
4. It leads to higher crop yield per unit area.
Which of the statements given above are correct regarding the Intensive Subsistence Agriculture?

so.t:
Subsistence agriculture is one of the important methods of the agricultural system where farmers consume most or all of the products grown locally. It can be classified into two types: Primitive Subsistence Agriculture and Intensive Subsistence Agriculture. Intensive Subsistence Agriculture is predominantly practiced in densely populated regions of monsoon Asia and has various features
Statement 1 is correct: Intensive subsistence agriculture can be dominated by wet paddy cultivation or other crops such as wheat, soybeans, barley, and sorghum, depending on the variations in geographical factors.
Statement 2 is incorrect: As Intensive subsistence agriculture is mainly found in densely populated regions, Land holdings are very small for agricultural activities.
Statements 3 and 4 are correct: Farmers work with the help of family labour leading to intensive use of land. Use of machinery is limited and most of the agricultural operations are done by manual labour. Farm yard manure is used to maintain the fertility of the soil. In this type of agriculture, the yield per unit area is high but per labour productivity is low.

q.t:
With reference to the seed sector in India, consider the following statements:
1. Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) of up to 100% is permitted in the seed sector under the automatic route subject to certain conditions.
2. No license is required for the export of any type of seed from India.
3. India’s seed industry has the largest market size in the world.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is correct: The Indian government has allowed up to 100% FDI in the seed sector through the automatic route subject to certain conditions regarding GM seeds. It means that foreign investors do not need prior approval from the government to invest in this sector.
This policy is designed to encourage foreign investment, technology transfer, and the introduction of new varieties of seeds, which can ultimately benefit Indian agriculture.
Statement 2 is incorrect: While the export of seeds has been simplified to encourage trade in the interest of farmers, a license is still required for certain types of seeds.
● Under the Export and Import Policy 2002-07, seeds of most crops have been placed under the Open General License (OGL), meaning they can be exported and imported freely.
● However, seeds of wild varieties, germplasm, breeder seeds, and onion seeds remain in the restricted category and require specific approval for export.
Statement 3 is incorrect: Although India's seed industry has grown significantly—from USD 2.75 billion in 2014 to USD 4.25 billion in 2023-24—it is not the largest in the world. The global seed industry is valued at USD 70 billion, with India ranking fifth, behind the United States, China, France, and Brazil.

q.t:
With reference to the All-India Rural Financial Inclusion Survey (NAFIS) (2021-22), conducted by NABARD, consider the following statements:
Between the Financial Year 2016-17 to 2021-22 period:
1. the average monthly income of rural households has decreased.
2. the share of food in the consumption basket of rural households has increased.
3. the average size of landholdings among rural households has decreased.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
NABARD released the results of its second All India Rural Financial Inclusion Survey (NAFIS) 2021-22, offering key insights into the economic and financial conditions of 1 lakh rural households in the post COVID period.
The survey provides valuable data on household income, expenditure, savings, debt, financial literacy, and access to credit and insurance. This follows NABARD’s first survey conducted for the agricultural year 2016-17, enabling a comparative analysis of rural financial trends over five years.
Statement 1 is incorrect: According to the survey, the average monthly income of rural households increased (not decreased) from ₹8,059 in 2016-17 to ₹12,698 in 2021-22. This represents a growth of 57.6% over five years and a nominal compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9.5%.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The data as per the survey indicates that the share of food in the total consumption basket has actually declined from 51% in 2016-17 to 47% in 2021-22.
A decrease in the share of food expenses suggests that rural households are now allocating a greater portion of their spending towards non-food items such as education, healthcare, etc.
Statement 3 is correct: The survey highlights that the average landholding size in rural areas declined from 1.08 hectares in 2016-17 to 0.74 hectares in 2021-22.
This reduction in landholding size could be due to factors such as population growth, land fragmentation, inheritance divisions, and increasing pressure on agricultural land.
Knowledge Base:
Other key highlights from the NAFIS 2021-22 survey:
1. Household Expenditure: Increased from ₹6,646 (2016-17) to ₹11,262 (2021-22).
2. Financial Savings: Annual average savings rose from ₹9,104 to ₹13,209; 66% of households saved in 2021-22 (up from 50.6%).
3. Outstanding Debt: Households with outstanding loans increased from 47.4% (2016-17) to 52% (2021 22).
4. Institutional Borrowing: Agricultural households borrowing from institutions rose from 60.5% to 75.5%; non-agricultural households from 56.7% to 72.7%.
5. Non-Institutional Borrowing: Declined for agricultural households from 30.3% to 23.4%.
6. Kisan Credit Card (KCC): Coverage significantly increased.
7. Insurance Coverage: Households with at least one insured member increased from 25.5% to 80.3%.
8. Financial Literacy: Share of financially literate respondents rose from 33.9% to 51.3%.

q.t:
In the context of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP), consider the following statements:
1. It is an attached office of the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public distribution.
2. It recommends Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane.
3. It determines the selling prices of fertilizers in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) is an advisory body under the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India. Established in 1965 as the Agricultural Prices Commission and renamed in 1985, its primary role is to recommend Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for various agricultural commodities to ensure remunerative prices to farmers and incentivize the adoption of modern agricultural practices.
Statement 1 is incorrect: The Commission for Agricultural Costs & Prices (CACP) is an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India (not Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public distribution).
Statement 2 is correct: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends the Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) of sugarcane to the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA). The FRP is a price that the government fixes for sugarcane, taking into account factors like the cost of production, market conditions, and fair returns for farmers.
Statement 3 is incorrect: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) does not directly determine the selling prices of fertilizers in India, but instead, it recommends Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for 23 agricultural crops. Assurance of a remunerative and stable price environment is considered very important for increasing agricultural production and productivity since the market place for agricultural produce tends to be inherently unstable, which often inflict undue losses on the growers, even when they adopt the best available technology package and produce efficiently.
Knowledge Base:
CACP recommends MSPs of 23 commodities, which comprise 7 cereals (paddy, wheat, maize, sorghum, pearl millet, barley and ragi), 5 pulses (gram, tur, moong, urad, lentil), 7 oilseeds (groundnut, rapeseed mustard, soyabean, seasmum, sunflower, safflower, nigerseed), and 4 commercial crops (copra, sugarcane, cotton and raw jute).

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) in India:
1. It is implemented by the PGS-India, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
2. It involves the accreditation of certification bodies, standards for organic production and promotion of organic farming and marketing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
The National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) is a pivotal initiative by the Government of India, aiming to establish and promote standards for organic products, ensuring their credibility in both domestic and international markets.
Statement 1 is incorrect: The National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) is implemented by APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority), which is under the Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India.
At present in India, two types of certification systems exist namely 1. Third Party certification(NPOP) system which is governed by APEDA, Ministry of Commerce which is mainly focused for export purposes and 2. PGS-INDIA certification system which is governed by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare mainly focused for local/ domestic market purposes.
Statement 2 is correct: National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) involves the accreditation of Certification Bodies, standards for organic production, promotion of organic farming and marketing etc.
The NPOP standards for production and accreditation have been recognized by the European Commission and Switzerland for unprocessed plant products as equivalent to their country standards.
With these recognitions, Indian organic products duly certified by the accredited certification bodies of India are accepted by the importing countries.

q.t:
Consider the following sources of irrigation:
1. Canals
2. Tanks
3. Tube wells
Which of the following represents the correct ascending order of sources of irrigation in India, based on the net irrigated area they cover?

so.t:
Exp) Option b is the correct answer.
Irrigation is the artificial application of water to crops to meet their water requirements. The primary sources of irrigation include wells, ponds, lakes, canals, tube wells, and dams. In India, the net irrigated area during 2021-22 was recorded at 77.9 million hectares.
Option 2: Tank irrigation serves multiple purposes, including maintaining water supply to the command area and recharging groundwater levels. Tanks contribute 2.8% to the net irrigated area in agriculture.
Option 1: Canals are artificial channels constructed to transport water from one place to another. They account for 24.7% of the net irrigated area in India.
Option 3: Tube wells are deep wells that use pipes bored into the ground to access groundwater. A pump is used to draw water to the surface. Tube wells constitute 47.3% of the total irrigated area in India.
Hence the ascending order of sources of irrigation based on the net irrigated area is option b: 2-3-1 i.e. Tanks (2.8%) < Canals (24.7%) < Tube Wells (47.3%).

q.t:
With reference to Agriculture, consider the following:
1. Higher agricultural productivity
2. Reduced pesticide usage
3. Better water use efficiency
4. Early identification of plant diseases
5. Reduced labour costs
6. Lower upfront investment
How many of the above are the advantages of Precision farming over conventional methods?

so.t:
Exp) Option c is the correct answer.
Precision farming is an approach that focuses on using inputs in exact quantities to achieve higher average yields compared to traditional farming methods. The potential of precision farming technologies includes Internet of Things (IoT), remote sensing, and smart irrigation systems, which work together to optimize water usage and enable real-time environmental monitoring.
Option 1 is correct: Precise agriculture inputs (like fertilizers, water) determined scientifically through analysis of data captured by sensors enhances the agriculture output and promotes the yield.
Option 2 is correct: Precision farming helps in the targeted application of pesticides, reducing their overall use and minimizing environmental impact.
Option 3 is correct: Precision farming employs technologies like smart irrigation systems that optimize water use, reducing wastage and improving efficiency.
Option 4 is correct: Precision farming includes techniques like sensors, drones, and satellite imaging to monitor plant health, allowing for early detection of issues and reducing crop loss.
Option 5 is correct: Automation and data-driven decision-making in precision farming can lead to a reduction in labor requirements, as fewer people are needed to manage tasks like irrigation, fertilizing, and pest control.
Option 6 is incorrect: Precision farming relies on specialized tools like GPS-guided tools, sensors, drones, and data analysis software. These can be expensive to buy and maintain. While precision farming can save money in the long run by optimizing inputs, the upfront investment can be significant, making it potentially more expensive than traditional farming methods initially.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the European Parliament (EP):
1. It is the only directly elected transnational assembly in the world.
2. It cannot propose laws but can only negotiate laws proposed by the European Commission.
3. The Members of the European Parliament cannot hold positions in national governments.
4. All European Union countries have equal representation in EP.
How many of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
● The European Parliament (EP) is the only directly elected transnational assembly in the world. The Members of the EP represent the interests of citizens of the 27 countries of the European Union (EU). So, statement 1 is correct.
○ Its primary functions include negotiating EU laws with the member state governments, represented by the European Council.
● The EP also approves the EU budget and votes on international agreements and enlargements of the bloc. It also has the power to approve or reject the appointment of the European Commission president. Unlike national parliaments, the EP does not have the right to propose laws but can only negotiate those proposed by the executive European Commission. So, statement 2 is correct.
○ The EP comprises 720 Members (MEPs) elected every five years. The MEPs then elect their president for two and a half years.
● Elected individuals cannot hold functions in national governments or other political bodies such as the EU Commission. So, statement 3 is correct.
● The number of MEPs elected in each country differs from 96 in Germany to 6 in Malta, Cyprus and Luxembourg. So, statement 4 is not correct.
So, only three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Term
 Description
1.
 Greedflation
 Businesses increase their goods prices by more than their costs have risen
2.
 Shrinkflation
 Size or quantity of the product is reduced while the price remains the same
3.
 Skimpflation
 Reduction in the quality of the product while the price remains the same
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?

so.t:
Inflation is a gradual loss of purchasing power, reflected in a broad rise in prices for goods and services over time. The companies are adapting in ways that have given rise to some additional ways to understand behaviour during an inflationary period:
● Greedflation: It is an increase in the price of goods and services caused by businesses increasing their prices by more than their costs have risen. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● Shrinkflation: It occurs when companies reduce the size or quantity of a product while keeping the price the same. Often, the goal is to shrink the packaging in such a way that the consumer doesn’t notice the change. So, pair 2 is correctly matched.
● Skimpflation: It occurs when companies reduce the quality of a product while keeping the price the same. For example, a company might use cheaper ingredients or lower-quality manufacturing processes to make a product.
So, pair 3 is correctly matched. So, all three of the pairs given above are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
● Excuseflation is when companies raise prices and blame it on inflation, even when inflation is not the main cause of the price increase.

q.t:
Consider the following countries:
1. Angola
2. Botswana
3. Namibia
4. Kenya
5. Zimbabwe
How many of the above are part of the Kavango Zambezi Trans-Frontier Conservation Area (KAZA-TFCA)?

so.t:
● The Kavango-Zambezi Trans-Frontier Conservation Area (KAZA-TFCA) is a wildlife sanctuary straddling five southern African nations (Angola, Botswana, Namibia, Zambia and Zimbabwe) that share common borders along the Okavango and Zambezi River basins. These nations, together with South Africa, are home to more than two-thirds of the African elephant population of about 450,000. So, points 1, 2, 3 and 5 are correct.
○ Kenya is not a part of KAZA-TFCA. So, point 4 is not correct.
● KAZA-2024-Heads of State Summit was held recently in the Zambia. The delegates cited an unreasonableness on part of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species (CITES) to maintain a blanket ban on ivory trade, thereby denying the five countries the benefit of monetising their huge elephant resources.
So, only four of the above are part of the Kavango-Zambezi Trans-Frontier Conservation Area (KAZA TFCA). Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following statements is/are correct with reference to ‘Ikat’ weaving art?
1. It is a dyeing technique used to create a distinct style of textile patterns.
2. It creates surface designs in the yarns rather than on the finished cloth.
3. It is mainly practised in Uttar Pradesh.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● ‘Ikat’ is a method for colouring fabric in patterns by resist-dyeing that creates a distinct style of textile patterns. So, statement 1 is correct.
● It is not applied to the surface of a finished fabric, nor is it woven into the fabric structurally. Instead, parts of the yarns for the warp and/or weft are protected with a resist before the dyeing. The dye then colours the yarn everywhere except under the binding. After dyeing, the bindings are removed and the pattern appears undyed on a coloured ground. So, statement 2 is correct.
● It primarily belongs to Odisha, where it has been conferred with the Geographical Indication (GI) tag by Government of India. Subarnapur district of Odisha is home to several weaver communities known for the unique ‘Ikat’, also known as Bomkai design.
So, statement 3 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding an anticipatory bail:
1. It cannot be issued in the case of non-bailable offences.
2. It can be issued by both the Session Courts and High Courts.
3. No regular bail is granted when an anticipatory bail is granted by higher courts and matter is pending.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Anticipatory bail means bail in anticipation of an arrest. Any person who apprehends arrest under a non bailable offence in India can apply for anticipatory bail. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The ability of the High Court and Sessions Court to grant anticipatory bail is covered under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023. The court has to consider the gravity of the accusations and criminal history of the accused in order to decide whether to reject the anticipatory bail application or to give an interim relief order. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Recently, Supreme Court, in Rukmani mahato vs. State of Jharkhand has directed trial courts to not grant regular bail to an accused, if he/she has already obtained an interim anticipatory bail by a superior court and the matter is still pending before the higher court.
So, statement 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: it was formed during the late Neoproterozoic era by massive lava eruptions
it is situated in the Campanian volcanic arc known for highly fluidic lava
it is formed with a group of volcanic mountains which keeps erupting frequently
it sits on the Mid-Atlantic ridge where the Eurasian and North American plates create volcanic rift zones

q.t: Iceland is one of the most volcanically active regions because:

so.t:
● Volcanoes are openings, or vents where lava, tephra (small rocks) and steam erupt onto the Earth’s surface. Volcanoes can be on land and in the ocean. They are formed when material significantly hotter than its surroundings is erupted onto the surface of the Earth.
● Iceland is one of the most volcanically active regions on the planet. The major reason for this is that Iceland sits on the Mid-Atlantic ridge (part of the longest mountain range in the world) in the North Atlantic Ocean, where the Eurasian and North American plates are moving apart a few centimetres every year. This produces volcanic rift zones, regions where the Earth’s crust is being pulled apart and fractured, and here molten rock, or magma, rises up and some reaches the surface and erupts as lava.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the NOTA option used in elections in India:
1. It was used for the first time in the general elections of 2009.
2. The votes cast to NOTA are counted but are considered ‘invalid votes’.
3. It is available in both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha elections.
4. The votes cast to NOTA do not change the outcome of an election.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● NOTA (None of the Above) is a voting option on the ballot that allows voters to indicate disapproval of all contesting candidates without choosing any of them. It gives them the right to reject while maintaining the secrecy of their decision.
● NOTA was used for the first time in the 2013 Assembly elections in five states- Chhattisgarh, Mizoram, Rajasthan, Delhi and Madhya Pradesh and later in the 2014 General Elections. It was introduced into the electoral process following the 2013 Supreme Court directive in the PUCL versus Union of India case. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The Election Commission of India clarified that votes cast as NOTA are counted, but are considered ‘invalid votes’. So, statement 2 is correct.
● The Supreme Court scrapped the use of NOTA option in the Rajya Sabha elections. The court held that the NOTA option is meant only for universal adult suffrage and direct elections and not polls held by the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote as done in the Rajya Sabha. So, statement 3 is not correct.
● Even if NOTA votes get the most number of votes in a constituency, the next candidate with the second-most number of votes is declared the winner. Therefore, votes made to NOTA do not change the outcome of an election. So, statement 4 is correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Microalgae has applications in which of the following fields?
1. Vaccine development
2. Drug delivery carriers
3. Production of biofertilizers
4. Production of biofuels
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● Microalgae is the source of marine origin compounds such as carbohydrates, peptides, lipids, and carotenoids. These have anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties in biomedicine and pharmaceutical biotechnology.
